parsed_review.summary: CP00-232 is a descriptor-only closeout pack covering RP06.P09.M05.S09-S18 (Permission And Audit Binding, 10 rows: severity taxonomy, go/no-go verdict format, finding routing map, human approval summary, Claude review packet, closeout criteria, PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK closeout notes, next-RP dependency). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level evidence supports closeout. Pack binding (registry.js:602) is consistent across manifest, closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-232 entry), and the contract (current_pack + embedded review_audit_binding_descriptor at lines 55991-56448): risk A, 10 units, range S09-S18, upstream CP00-231, next CP00-233/S19. All 7 declared public exports are defined (createDmsCoreCp232ReviewAuditBindingDescriptor/CaseSet in service.js; validateDmsCoreCp232Coverage, validateDmsCoreCp232ReviewAuditBindingDescriptor, createDmsCoreCp232HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff in validators.js) and re-exported through index.js wildcard. The case-set builder emits exactly the 10 M05 rows with the correct row extras (severity_levels P0-P3; verdict_values PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK with claude_final_approval_claimed=false; human_approval_route_required_before_runtime=true; claude_review_packet.read_only=true; block_closeout_note.blocks_closeout_descriptor=true), and the generated structure matches the committed contract embed. Validators enforce coverage (pack id, risk, unit count, first/last unit, RP06-only, deliverable/phase/micro distribution, section rows) and descriptor invariants (descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe, no raw payload), plus authority-boundary and no-write attestations. Test coverage (model.test.js 3 CP232 tests) and the validate script assert binding validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundary, evidence packets, and handoff. command-evidence.json records all 18 commands at exit 0 (node --check/--test, rp06:dms-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check), and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid JSON result). Boundaries are intact: production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review (no premature production claim); Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime opens in CP00-233); Hermes patterns are deterministic packet/receipt only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false); no real data, no runtime permission/audit evaluation, no state writes, no object storage/OCR/search/email/Citation Ledger/Loop runtime; CP178+ invalid-review blockers (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_or_enterprise_trust_claim) are present. Changed-file scope correctly isolates active implementation refs from unrelated dirty files, which are preserved, not attributed to this pack. Post-review evidence artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is the expected pre-review state per the hardening sequence and is explicitly noted in command-evidence.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one non-blocking P3 observation about validation depth is recorded below.

findings.title: Contract drift check for CP232 descriptor validates type string only, not embedded row structure
findings.details: validateDmsCoreCp232ReviewAuditBindingDescriptor (invoked by scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs:759 with the committed dmsContract) only compares contractProjection.review_audit_binding_descriptor.descriptor (the type string 'DmsCoreCp232ReviewAuditBindingDescriptor') against the generated descriptor. It does not deep-compare the committed contract's embedded rows and attestations (contracts/dms-core-contract.json lines 55991-56448) against the code-generated structure. A hand-edit to the committed contract embed (e.g., flipping a descriptor_only or runtime_execution flag in an embedded row) would not be caught by this check, since the validator only asserts the freshly generated descriptor. I manually verified during this review that the committed embed is in sync with the generated structure, so there is no actual drift in CP00-232. This is a long-standing cross-pack validation pattern, not introduced by CP00-232.
findings.recommendation: Optionally add a deep-equality assertion between the committed contract embed (dmsContract.review_audit_binding_descriptor) and the generated descriptor to detect future hand-edit drift in the committed contract snapshot. Non-blocking for CP00-232 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as pre-existing cross-pack validation pattern. Embedded contract descriptor (lines 55991-56448) manually verified in sync with the code-generated structure during this review; no drift present. No action required for CP00-232 closeout.
